questions.question_text: The relationship between a qualifying broker and associate broker must be documented in:
questions.explanation: NM requires a written agreement between the QB and AB that specifies the nature of the relationship, including whether the AB is an employee or independent contractor.

questions.question_text: For an associate broker to qualify as an independent contractor, which factor must be present?
questions.correct_answer: Compensation based on production, not hours
distractors: An office provided by the qualifying broker
A fixed monthly salary with bonuses
questions.explanation: IC status requires compensation tied to production (commissions), not hours worked. Other IC factors include a written agreement, broker not controlling work methods, and AB paying own taxes.

questions.question_text: A qualifying broker classifies an associate broker as an independent contractor but dictates their daily schedule and work methods. This arrangement:
questions.correct_answer: May not qualify as an IC relationship
distractors: Is standard IC practice in real estate
Only matters for tax filing purposes
questions.explanation: True IC status requires the broker not to control the means and methods of work. Dictating daily schedules contradicts IC criteria, regardless of what the written agreement says.

questions.question_text: An associate broker is classified as an independent contractor. The qualifying broker's supervisory obligation is:
questions.correct_answer: The same as for an employee AB
questions.explanation: Regardless of whether the AB is an employee or IC, the qualifying broker retains full supervisory responsibility and must ensure compliance with all laws and Commission rules.

questions.question_text: Who is responsible for paying income taxes on an associate broker classified as an independent contractor?
distractors: NMREC deducts taxes from renewal fees
The state Regulation and Licensing Department
questions.explanation: One of the hallmarks of IC status is that the associate broker is responsible for their own taxes. The QB does not withhold taxes for independent contractor ABs.

questions.question_text: An associate broker places a social media ad for a listing that shows only their name and phone number. This ad is:
distractors: Permitted if the post links to the brokerage
Allowed for personal social media accounts
questions.explanation: All advertising, including social media, must include the qualifying broker's name as registered with NMREC. An ad without broker identification is a blind ad and is prohibited.

questions.question_text: A real estate team creates a yard sign with the team name in large letters and the qualifying broker's name in small print at the bottom. Is this compliant?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, if the QB name is included
distractors: No, team names are never permitted on signs
No, the QB name must be largest on the sign
Yes, but only for residential properties
questions.explanation: Team names are permitted in NM advertising, and the AB's or team's name may appear, but the qualifying broker's name must also be present. The AB/team name must not be more prominent than the QB's name.

questions.question_text: Which element must appear in ALL NM real estate advertising?
questions.explanation: All advertising must include the qualifying broker's name as registered with NMREC. License numbers, approval stamps, and physical addresses are not universally required in ads.

questions.question_text: A broker advertises a property as having "mountain views" when the view is actually of a parking lot. This advertising is:
questions.correct_answer: A violation of truthful advertising rules
distractors: Permitted if the ad says views may vary
Allowed if the property has partial views
questions.explanation: All advertising must be truthful and not misleading. Claiming mountain views when the actual view is a parking lot is a clear misrepresentation.

questions.question_text: An associate broker's name appears on a billboard in font twice the size of the qualifying broker's name. This is:
questions.correct_answer: A violation of advertising prominence rules
distractors: Acceptable if both names are present
Allowed for billboards but not print ads
questions.explanation: The associate broker's name must not be more prominent than the qualifying broker's name in any advertising. Having the AB's name twice as large violates this rule.

questions.question_text: How long must a qualifying broker retain transaction records in New Mexico?
questions.explanation: All trust and custodial account records and transaction records must be retained for 6 years after the completion of a transaction.

questions.question_text: Which documents must be included in a qualifying broker's transaction records?
questions.explanation: Required records include listing agreements, purchase contracts, trust account records, closing statements, and correspondence — a comprehensive set of transaction documentation.

questions.question_text: A qualifying broker keeps all records in a cloud-based digital system. Under NM rules, this is:
questions.correct_answer: Acceptable if they meet Commission standards
distractors: Prohibited since paper records are mandatory
Permitted only with prior NMREC approval
questions.explanation: Digital records are acceptable as long as they meet Commission requirements for accessibility, completeness, and the ability to produce them for inspection.

questions.question_text: NMREC requests to inspect a brokerage's transaction records. How quickly must the qualifying broker provide access?
questions.correct_answer: Records must be readily accessible at all times
distractors: Within 30 business days of the request
Within 10 calendar days of written notice
At the next scheduled NMREC audit date
questions.explanation: Records must be readily accessible for Commission inspection at any time. NMREC can audit records without prior notice, so brokers must maintain organized, accessible files.

questions.question_text: A transaction closed 5 years ago. The qualifying broker wants to destroy the records to save storage space. This would be:
questions.correct_answer: Premature because the 6-year period has not passed
distractors: Acceptable since transactions over 3 years old can be purged
Permitted if digital copies exist elsewhere
questions.explanation: Records must be retained for 6 years after completion of a transaction. Destroying them at 5 years would violate the retention requirement by one year.
